data.topic: running tests cli commands headed headless parallel sharding
data.content:
### Shard Test Suite Execution Across Multiple Machines - Bash

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Demonstrates how to split a Playwright test suite into four shards using the --shard command line option. Each shard runs one fourth of the tests independently, allowing parallel execution across different machines or CI jobs to achieve 4x faster test completion.

```bash
npx playwright test --shard=1/4
npx playwright test --shard=2/4
npx playwright test --shard=3/4
npx playwright test --shard=4/4
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Disable Test Parallelization CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command runs Playwright tests sequentially by setting the number of workers to one. Useful for debugging tests that might have race conditions or dependencies.

```bash
npx playwright test --workers=1
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Actions workflow for parallel Playwright test sharding

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Sets up a GitHub Actions job that runs Playwright tests in parallel across 4 shards using matrix strategy. Each shard executes a portion of the test suite and uploads its blob report as an artifact for later merging. The workflow triggers on push and pull request events to main/master branches.

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  playwright-tests:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        shardIndex: [1, 2, 3, 4]
        shardTotal: [4]
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright browsers
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps

    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test --shard=${{ matrix.shardIndex }}/${{ matrix.shardTotal }}

    - name: Upload blob report to GitHub Actions Artifacts
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: blob-report-${{ matrix.shardIndex }}
        path: blob-report
        retention-days: 1
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Playwright Tests Across Multiple Machines

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Shard Playwright tests to distribute execution across multiple machines. This command-line option specifies which shard of the test suite to run, useful for scaling test execution.

```bash
npx playwright test --shard=2/3
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Tests in Headed Browser CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command executes Playwright tests in browsers with a visible UI, as opposed to the default headless mode. Useful for visual debugging and observation.

```bash
# Run tests in headed browsers
npx playwright test --headed
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests with Blob Reporter via CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-reporters-js.md

Execute Playwright tests and generate a Blob report directly from the command line. This command outputs the report to the default 'blob-report' directory, facilitating subsequent report merging for sharded test runs.

```bash
npx playwright test --reporter=blob
```

--------------------------------

### Merge Shard Reports into Single HTML Report - Bash

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Command to merge blob reports from multiple test shards into a single consolidated HTML report. Requires all blob report files to be placed in a common directory, then uses the merge-reports command to generate a unified HTML report in the playwright-report directory.

```bash
npx playwright merge-reports --reporter html ./all-blob-reports
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Playwright Tests in GitLab CI with parallel:matrix (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

Shows how to use 'parallel:matrix' in GitLab CI to run Playwright tests with different project and shard combinations. This example creates multiple parallel jobs by combining various project values with several shard values, useful for comprehensive matrix testing.

```yaml
stages:
  - test

tests:
  stage: test
  image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v%%VERSION%%-noble
  parallel:
    matrix:
      - PROJECT: ['chromium', 'webkit']
        SHARD: ['1/10', '2/10', '3/10', '4/10', '5/10', '6/10', '7/10', '8/10', '9/10', '10/10']
  script:
    - npm ci
    - npx playwright test --project=$PROJECT --shard=$SHARD
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests via dotnet CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/intro-csharp.md

This command line snippet shows how to execute Playwright end-to-end tests using the .NET CLI. It runs all tests defined in the project, by default in headless Chromium mode. The browser and launch configuration can be adjusted using environment variables or configuration files.

```bash
dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Run Tests with New Chromium Headless Mode via CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-csharp.md

Executes tests using the new Chromium headless mode by passing browser and channel configurations as command-line arguments.

```bash
dotnet test -- Playwright.BrowserName=chromium Playwright.LaunchOptions.Channel=chromium
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Multiple Test Files/Directories CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command allows executing tests from multiple specified files or directories. It's useful for running a subset of tests grouped by feature or area.

```bash
# Run a set of test files
npx playwright test tests/todo-page/ tests/landing-page/
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Pytest tests with CLI arguments

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-python.md

Execute Playwright end-to-end tests using the Pytest CLI. This command launches tests in WebKit browser in headed mode, making the browser UI visible during execution.

```bash
pytest --browser webkit --headed
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Single Test File CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command runs a specific Playwright test file by providing its path. Useful for isolating and debugging individual test suites.

```bash
# Run a single test file
npx playwright test tests/todo-page.spec.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Limit Playwright Test Workers via Command Line

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

This command allows you to specify the maximum number of parallel worker processes for Playwright Test execution directly from the command line. This is useful for controlling resource usage during test runs.

```bash
npx playwright test --workers 4
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Playwright test suite across multiple machines (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/best-practices-js.md

This command demonstrates how to shard a Playwright test suite, allowing it to be distributed and executed across multiple machines. The '--shard=1/3' argument specifies that this execution will run the first third of the entire test suite. This feature is crucial for scaling test execution in CI/CD environments by splitting the workload.

```bash
npx playwright test --shard=1/3
```

```bash
yarn playwright test --shard=1/3
```

```bash
pnpm exec playwright test --shard=1/3
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests in Headed Mode (C#)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-csharp.md

These commands allow running Playwright tests with a visible browser window, rather than the default headless mode. This is useful for visual debugging and observation of test execution. It involves setting an environment variable before invoking `dotnet test`.

```bash
HEADED=1 dotnet test
```

```batch
set HEADED=1
dotnet test
```

```powershell
$env:HEADED="1"
dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run All Tests CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command executes all Playwright tests configured in the project. It's the simplest way to initiate a full test run.

```bash
# Run all tests
npx playwright test
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Playwright Tests in GitLab CI with parallel keyword (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

Demonstrates how to shard Playwright tests across multiple parallel jobs in GitLab CI using the 'parallel' keyword. This configuration splits tests into a specified number of smaller jobs, with each job using a specific shard index and total count.

```yaml
stages:
  - test

tests:
  stage: test
  image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v%%VERSION%%-noble
  parallel: 7
  script:
    - npm ci
    - npx playwright test --shard=$CI_NODE_INDEX/$CI_NODE_TOTAL
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Parallel Playwright Test Execution with Multiple Workers (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-csharp.md

These commands demonstrate how to configure different .NET test runners (MSTest, NUnit, xUnit) to execute Playwright tests in parallel using multiple worker processes. This significantly reduces total test execution time for large test suites.

```bash
dotnet test -- NUnit.NumberOfTestWorkers=5
```

```bash
dotnet test -- MSTest.Parallelize.Workers=5
```

```bash
dotnet test -- xUnit.MaxParallelThreads=5
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests in Headed Mode with Xvfb on Linux

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

Instructions for running Playwright tests in headed mode on Linux CI agents using 'xvfb-run'. This command wraps the test execution to provide a virtual display for browsers that require a graphical environment, ensuring headed tests can run without a physical display.

```bash
xvfb-run npx playwright test
```

```bash
xvfb-run pytest
```

```bash
xvfb-run mvn test
```

```bash
xvfb-run dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Playwright Tests and CLI with Gradle

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-java.md

These `bash` commands demonstrate how to run Playwright tests and invoke Playwright's command-line interface tools using Gradle. `./gradlew run` executes the application, which typically includes tests, while `./gradlew playwright --args="help"` utilizes the custom task to interact with the Playwright CLI.

```bash
./gradlew run
```

```bash
./gradlew playwright --args="help"
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Tests with Default Configuration using npx

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-js.md

Executes all Playwright tests as configured in playwright.config file. Tests run in parallel and headless mode by default across all configured browsers, with results displayed in the terminal.

```bash
npx playwright test
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Blob Reporter for CI Test Sharding - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Configuration for enabling blob reporter in Playwright when running tests in CI environments. The blob reporter captures test results and attachments (traces, screenshots) that can later be merged from multiple shards into a unified report.

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  testDir: './tests',
  reporter: process.env.CI ? 'blob' : 'html',
});
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Fully Parallel Test Execution in Playwright Config

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

Configures Playwright Test to run tests in fully parallel mode, where tests within a single file are also parallelized (not just between files). This can significantly speed up test execution. Can also be enabled via --fully-parallel command line flag.

```javascript
export default {
  fullyParallel: true,
};
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Pytest Tests (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/intro-python.md

Executes all Playwright tests within the current working directory or its subdirectories using the `pytest` command. Tests run in headless mode by default, meaning no browser UI opens, and results are displayed in the terminal.

```bash
pytest
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Test Sharding in Playwright Test (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-api/class-testconfig.md

Enables sharding of test execution, allowing tests to be distributed across multiple processes or machines. This property takes an object with `total` (the total number of shards) and `current` (the one-based index of the shard to execute). It's crucial for parallelizing large test suites in CI environments.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  shard: { total: 10, current: 3 },
});
```

--------------------------------

### CircleCI Sharding Configuration for Playwright Tests in JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

CircleCI job configuration demonstrating test sharding with parallelism for JavaScript Playwright tests. Uses CIRCLE_NODE_INDEX incremented by 1 (since CircleCI uses 0-based indexing) to properly distribute tests across 4 parallel containers. This configuration ensures efficient test distribution while respecting CircleCI's indexing system.

```yaml
playwright-job-name:
  executor: pw-noble-development
  parallelism: 4
  steps:
    - run: SHARD="$((${CIRCLE_NODE_INDEX}+1))"; npx playwright test --shard=${SHARD}/${CIRCLE_NODE_TOTAL}
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Tests for Specific Project CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command targets and runs Playwright tests only for a specified project configuration. Projects are defined in the Playwright config file, e.g., 'chromium'.

```bash
# Run tests for a specific project
npx playwright test --project=chromium
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Tests by Title (Grep) CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command filters and runs Playwright tests whose titles match the provided string or regular expression. It uses the `-g` or `--grep` option for filtering.

```bash
# Run tests by title
npx playwright test -g "add a todo item"
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright tests in parallel with Pytest

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-python.md

Execute Playwright tests concurrently across multiple processes using Pytest, significantly speeding up test execution. This functionality requires the `pytest-xdist` plugin to be installed. The `--numprocesses` flag specifies the number of parallel workers to use.

```bash
pytest --numprocesses 2
```

--------------------------------

### Merge Playwright blob reports into single HTML report in GitHub Actions

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Configures a dependent GitHub Actions job that runs after all test shards complete, downloading all blob report artifacts and merging them into a consolidated HTML report. The job executes even if some shards fail, and uploads the final HTML report with a 14-day retention period.

```yaml
jobs:
...
  merge-reports:
    # Merge reports after playwright-tests, even if some shards have failed
    if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
    needs: [playwright-tests]

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci

    - name: Download blob reports from GitHub Actions Artifacts
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
      with:
        path: all-blob-reports
        pattern: blob-report-*
        merge-multiple: true

    - name: Merge into HTML Report
      run: npx playwright merge-reports --reporter html ./all-blob-reports

    - name: Upload HTML report
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: html-report--attempt-${{ github.run_attempt }}
        path: playwright-report
        retention-days: 14
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Tests CLI Syntax

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This snippet shows the basic command line syntax for executing Playwright tests. It includes options and allows filtering tests by specific patterns or files.

```bash
npx playwright test [options] [test-filter...]
```

--------------------------------

### Set Maximum Test Failures via Command Line - Bash

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Limits the number of failed tests using the --max-failures flag when running Playwright tests. When this limit is reached, Playwright stops execution and skips remaining tests, saving resources on broken test suites. This is particularly useful in CI/CD environments.

```bash
npx playwright test --max-failures=10
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright tests in parallel within a file using `test.describe.parallel`

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

This snippet demonstrates how to configure Playwright Test to execute tests within the same file in parallel. By default, tests run sequentially. Using `test.describe.parallel(title, callback)` allows independent tests to run concurrently, significantly speeding up execution times for larger test suites.

```javascript
test.describe.parallel('group', () => {
  test('runs in parallel 1', async ({ page }) => {
  });
  test('runs in parallel 2', async ({ page }) => {
  });
});
```

--------------------------------

### Run Pytest with Chromium Channel for New Headless Mode

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-python.md

This command demonstrates how to execute Playwright tests using `pytest` with the new Chromium headless mode. By specifying `--browser-channel chromium`, users opt into the more authentic and reliable headless browser environment.

```bash
pytest test_login.py --browser-channel chromium
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Run Tests at Specific Line CLI

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-cli-js.md

This command executes tests starting from a specific line number within a given test file. Ideal for focusing on a particular test case or section during development.

```bash
# Run tests at a specific line
npx playwright test my-spec.ts:42
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright tests to run in parallel mode

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-api/class-test.md

This snippet shows how to set the execution mode for all tests in a file or enclosing scope to `parallel` using `test.describe.configure`. This causes tests to run concurrently using parallel workers, improving execution speed.

```javascript
// Run all the tests in the file concurrently using parallel workers.
test.describe.configure({ mode: 'parallel' });
test('runs in parallel 1', async ({ page }) => {});
test('runs in parallel 2', async ({ page }) => {});
```

--------------------------------

### Azure Pipelines Sharded Test Execution Strategy for Playwright - JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

Advanced Azure Pipelines configuration implementing parallel test execution using sharding strategy. Defines a matrix strategy that distributes tests across three browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) with each browser split into three shards, resulting in nine parallel jobs for optimal test performance.

```yaml
trigger:
- main

pool:
  vmImage: ubuntu-latest

strategy:
  matrix:
    chromium-1:
      project: chromium
      shard: 1/3
    chromium-2:
      project: chromium
      shard: 2/3
    chromium-3:
      project: chromium
      shard: 3/3
    firefox-1:
      project: firefox
      shard: 1/3
    firefox-2:
      project: firefox
      shard: 2/3
    firefox-3:
      project: firefox
      shard: 3/3
    webkit-1:
      project: webkit
      shard: 1/3
    webkit-2:
      project: webkit
      shard: 2/3
    webkit-3:
      project: webkit
      shard: 3/3
steps:
- task: UseNode@1
  inputs:
    version: '22'
  displayName: 'Install Node.js'

- script: npm ci
  displayName: 'npm ci'
- script: npx playwright install --with-deps
  displayName: 'Install Playwright browsers'
- script: npx playwright test --project=$(project) --shard=$(shard)
  displayName: 'Run Playwright tests'
  env:
    CI: 'true'
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright tests in headed mode with Pytest

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-python.md

Execute Playwright tests using Pytest, explicitly opening a browser window to visually observe the test execution. The browser remains open until the tests are completed. Use the `--headed` flag to enable this mode.

```bash
pytest --headed
```

params.topic: running tests cli commands headed headless parallel sharding
